what_it_does: This file is an interpretive perspective that reads the original intent behind design/_system and uses that lens to propose how the ledger/design/_system fusion should be shaped, arguing for a unified addressed substrate that holds both code and product domains.
observations: Frames design/_system not as a design-tool folder but as an early attempt at a single addressed, queryable graph of product and code.
Treats the ledger and design/_system as two halves of one idea, approached from code and product faces respectively.
Recommends a synthesis rather than a flat unidirectional merge in either direction.
Proposes adding product node domains (ui_address, feature, view, journey) and product edge kinds to the ledger graph.
Proposes turning design/_system analyzers (refcheck, symbols, navgraph, coverage, etc.) into SQL views/queries over the unified graph.
Proposes fusing mechanisms.json with the ledger's derived-by-query principle to create an open check-registry of graph queries.
Distinguishes curated authoring surfaces (addresses.json, Feature & Function Inventory, register.json) from derived outputs (refcheck-report, code-symbols, code-edges, element-map, coverage map).
Identifies the biggest risk as over-supersession: assuming the product layer can be deterministically extracted like code symbols.
Cites LEDGER-SPEC §8 as already anticipating address-spine integration as a follow-up.
Emphasizes one resolver for ui://, run://, and code:// over one substrate.
standouts: The 'shadow of the idea' framing: one addressed substrate rendered to Tim's recognition.
Recommendation to generalize the ledger from code-only into a substrate that also holds the product domain, with design analyzers becoming queries/views.
Warning against over-supersession: the product/curated layer must be authored and ingested, never extracted.
The proposed fusion of mechanisms.json's open check-registry with the ledger's derived-by-query analysis layer.
conventions: Follows the perspective frontmatter schema with type, register, status, lens, subject, coverage, and sources.
Uses numbered sections and markdown headings.
Makes heavy inline citations to source files and coverage documents.
Is explicitly opinionated by design, as declared in the opening.
Uses long-form argumentative prose rather than terse specification.
concerns: Strongly normative language for a 'descriptive' perspective, though it declares itself opinionated.
Relies on many cross-file citations that cannot be verified from this file alone.
The proposed unified schema is conceptual; exact table shapes, migrations, and ingestion mechanics are not specified.
The end 'one-line statement' largely repeats the preceding recommendation.
No explicit reconciliation with sibling perspectives, by design, which may complicate orchestration.
notes: An opinionated fusion perspective arguing the ledger should absorb the product address space as first-class graph nodes while keeping curated design registries as authored inputs, not extracted data.
questions: What is the exact schema for the proposed product node tables and how do they relate to ledger.entry/symbol/edge?
Who or what performs deterministic ingestion from addresses.json and register.json into the graph?
What is the precise ordering mechanism for journey step edges?
What is the migration path for retiring the four generated JSON files and their consumers?
How does the model_augmentation / Layer B seam concretely attach to product nodes and checks?
What does 'Tim's recognition' mean operationally for the recognition surface?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to provide one lens's independent interpretive take; the file does that, but it also functions as a full architectural recommendation, consistent with its declared opinionated-by-design stance.
apparent_themes: One addressed substrate / universal coordinate space
intention_signals: It is trying to shape the fusion so that design/_system's product, curated, and recognition layers are preserved inside a deeper ledger substrate, producing a single queryable graph spanning both code and product rather than letting either system subsume the other.
novelty: It reframes design/_system as an early expression of the same addressed-substrate idea the ledger later built from the code side, and uses that reframing to argue for synthesis rather than takeover; it also names 'over-supersession' as a central risk.
contribution: It contributes an interpretive lens of original intent, a synthesis recommendation to generalize the ledger graph into a code-plus-product substrate, a proposed set of product node and edge kinds, a checks-as-queries pattern combining mechanisms.json with the ledger, a curated-vs-derived boundary, and an explicit risk framing around over-supersession.
summary_for_embedding: A perspective document interpreting the original intent of design/_system as an addressed substrate for both product and code, and recommending that the ledger be generalized to include ui:// addresses, features, views, and journeys as first-class graph nodes while curated product registries remain authored inputs.
intention_for_embedding: To shape the ledger/design/_system fusion by arguing both systems were reaching for the same one-substrate idea and that the merge should realize a unified queryable graph rather than retire either half.
